citation: Kelly, Hart & Rose, J. Phys. Energy 3 (2021)
basis_as_published: beam energy per negative muon created (deuteron beam, tungsten target; G4Beamline); per muon PRODUCED; not wall-plug (eta_acc separate)
capture_scheme: pion production in W target; muons from pi- decay
derivation: as-published; no OURS-normalization (already GeV per muon). Optimized G4Beamline point: beam energy 3.61 GeV / (pi-/muons per beam particle) = 4.70 GeV/muon (deuteron on W; 150 fusions/muon). Wall-plug = 4.70/eta_acc, eta_acc=0.18 kept separate (never folded).
source_locator: DOI 10.1088/2515-7655/abfb4b (open access); muon-cost result; Q_elec=14% at X_mu=150
notes: THE open-access anchor (G4Beamline, fully checkable). eta_acc=0.18 (PSI-measured, verified this session: '150 fusions per muon with 18% accelerator efficiency'); Q_elec=14% at X_mu=150 (abstract); recapture credit x2.5 (kinetic-energy recovery + tritium-breeding heat) in recapture_factor, NOT folded into 4.70; Q=0.83 pre-recapture (26.0 MeV/fusion x150 = 3.9 GeV heat).

basis_as_published: beam kinetic energy per muon (C_mu-); per stopped muon in the D-T target (in-target production, liquid rho_0); FLUKA Monte Carlo; not wall-plug
capture_scheme: pions produced in D-T target; muons from pi- decay stopped in the fuel
derivation: as-published beam-energy-per-muon at liquid density rho_0 (Table II, n-beam 3.5 GeV/c: C_mu = 7.8 +- 1.8 GeV); no OURS-normalization. Full design-study span: ~3 GeV ideal (all muons collected, intermediate beam) to ~8-10 GeV (liquid in-target: n 7.8, p 10.0, d 8.8 GeV).
source_locator: Table II p.879 (density rho_0, n beam 3.5 GeV/c); Conclusions p.880 (iii ~8 GeV liquid in-target; iv ~3 GeV ideal all-collected)
notes: Full-text-verified from the primary PDF this session (DOI 10.1209/0295-5075/4/8/003). Costs fall at sub-liquid density (0.5/0.2/0.1 rho_0 -> 4.3/2.6/2.0 GeV n-beam) but muCF needs liquid density; the liquid figure is the per-stopped-in-fuel basis. Paper notes agreement with Jandel et al. (CERN-TH 4703/87).

basis_as_published: energy to produce one negative muon (~5000 MeV, optimistic estimate); per muon PRODUCED; not wall-plug
capture_scheme: pions from nucleon-nucleon collisions; muons from pi- decay
derivation: as-published optimistic energy-to-produce-one-muon ~5000 MeV = 5.0 GeV (Abstract p.46; Sec.VII p.57); no OURS-normalization. Sec.VII.A (Petrov reactor) gives per-STOPPED-muon-in-D-T ~6 GeV (tritium beam) / ~8 GeV (deuterium beam).
source_locator: Abstract p.46; Sec.VII (Energy Production) p.57; Sec.VII.A p.58 (per-stopped-mu 6/8 GeV)
notes: Full-text-verified from the primary PDF this session (DOI 10.13182/FST94-A30300). 'a muon can be produced with current technology for an expenditure of ~5000 MeV ... X_mu ~ 250 would be a significant demonstration of scientific breakeven.'

derivation: not derived -- digit not pinned from the primary text (Jandel 1989 Muon Catal. Fusion not in hand).
notes: needs_verification: the Jandel 1989 Muon Catal. Fusion muon-cost design study is NOT in hand; the separately-held Jandel PLB 200, 565 (1988) thermal-spikes paper is a DIFFERENT document and is not its source. Bertin 1987 (p.879) notes its costs are 'in good agreement with Jandel et al.' [CERN-TH 4703/87] (~few-to-8 GeV design-study range) -- secondhand context only, never a pin.

citation: Acceleron Fusion / NK Labs (Newburg), ARPA-E Fusion Programs Annual Meeting, 8-9 July 2025
basis_as_published: energy per negative pion and muon EXITING the active target (GEANT4 sim); ion (triton) beam; per muon exiting (production, not stopped); not wall-plug
capture_scheme: active-target: pions produced + muons collected by fields inside target
derivation: as-published GEANT4-simulated 3.0 GeV per negative pion+muon exiting the active target (slide 6); system diagram (slide 5) uses 3 GeV/muon. No OURS-normalization.
source_locator: ARPA-E deck slide 6 (GEANT4 sim: 3.0 GeV per pi-/muon exiting target) + slide 5 (3 GeV/muon system diagram; 3.4 GeV net export)
notes: simulated, unvalidated, company slide (ARPA-E deck July 2025). NEVER headlines. Public deck live-fetched + archived this session; 'design can likely be improved with further engineering' (slide 6). Lowest T1 value but slide-tier.

citation: Muon-collider / neutrino-factory front end (proton driver + solenoid capture + ionization cooling)
basis_as_published: muons per proton, COLLECTED for a collider (NOT stopped in fuel): ~0.8 mu/p at capture (constant solenoid); ~0.045 mu / 8-GeV-proton after ionization cooling (HFOFO snake)
capture_scheme: solenoid pion capture + rf phase rotation + ionization cooling
derivation: implied, derived here: 8 GeV/proton / 0.045 mu/proton (post-cooling, HFOFO snake) = 178 GeV per COLLECTED muon; / 0.8 mu/proton at capture = 10 GeV. COLLECTED-not-stopped: these muons are cooled for a collider, never stopped in D-T fuel (per-stopped-in-fuel cost would be higher).
source_locator: PRAB 17, 070102 (2014) capture; HFOFO-snake front end arXiv:1612.08977; 8 GeV / 2-4 MW proton driver
notes: collected-not-stopped caveat (basis + here): a collider collects muons in a cooled beam, it does not stop them in fuel. Bridges T1 design studies to T3 operating facilities.

basis_as_published: 8 GeV protons, 8 kW production-target beam; ~1e10 muons/s stopped in the Al stopping target
capture_scheme: graded-field solenoid capture; muon transport solenoid; Al stopping target
derivation: implied, derived here: 8 kW / (1.602e-10 J/GeV) = 4.99e13 GeV/s beam / 1e10 stopped mu/s = 4993 ~ 5.0e3 GeV per stopped muon (equivalently 8 GeV/proton x ~624 protons/stopped-mu).
source_locator: Fermilab Mu2e public design (8 GeV, 8 kW; ~1e10 stopped mu/s; ~1 in 300 protons -> transport solenoid)
notes: operating facility; the GeV/stopped-mu is OUR derivation (no facility reports this quantity). Muons stopped in Al, not D-T fuel -- optimized for brightness/purity, not muons-per-watt.

basis_as_published: 8 GeV, 56 kW (7 uA) proton beam; muon yield ~0.0035 mu/proton in the muon-stopping-target region
capture_scheme: pion-capture solenoid (5 T); curved transport solenoid; Al stopping target
derivation: implied, derived here: 8 GeV/proton / 0.0035 mu/proton = 2286 ~ 2.3e3 GeV per stopped muon.
source_locator: PTEP 2013, 022C01 + COMET public design; muon yield ~0.0035 mu/p; 8 GeV / 56 kW / 7 uA
notes: operating facility; OUR derivation. Muons stopped in Al, not D-T fuel.

citation: MuSIC (RCNP Osaka), superconducting pion-capture muon source
basis_as_published: measured (10.4 +- 2.7)e5 muons per Watt of 400 MeV proton beam power (mu+ and mu-)
derivation: implied, derived here: 1 W = (1/1.602e-10) GeV/s = 6.242e9 GeV/s; / 10.4e5 mu/W = 6002 ~ 6.0e3 GeV per muon (= inverse of muons-per-watt; density-independent; mu+ and mu-, produced not stopped).
notes: world's most efficient measured muons-per-watt; even so ~6e3 GeV/muon. OUR derivation from the measured muons-per-watt. Produced (mu+ and mu-), not stopped in fuel.

citation: PSI HIMB (High-Intensity Muon Beams), HIPA 590 MeV
basis_as_published: 1.42 MW HIPA 590 MeV proton beam; HIMB design 1e10 mu+/s (surface muons, mu+ ONLY)
capture_scheme: slanted graphite target + solenoid capture; surface muons
derivation: implied, derived here: 1.42 MW / (1.602e-10 J/GeV) = 8.86e15 GeV/s / 1e10 mu+/s = 8.9e5 ~ 1e6 GeV per muon (mu+-ONLY). Range ~1e5-1e6: the full 1.42 MW HIPA beam is shared across stations, so per-HIMB attribution is an upper bound; even a 10% beam share gives ~1e5.
source_locator: EPJ Web Conf. (SSP2023) 01012; HIMB 1e10 mu+/s; HIPA 590 MeV / 1.42 MW
notes: mu+-ONLY (surface muons) -- irrelevant to muCF, which needs mu-; stated explicitly. Highest-tier value ~1e6 GeV/muon. OUR derivation; shared-beam attribution -> range 1e5-1e6.
